text:
There once was a wise old crow who knew the depth of the ocean. Every morning, he'd fly high above the water, scouting out the depths. One particular morning, he spotted a school of shimmering dolphins playing near some ancient seaweed.

Curiosity getting the better of him, he tilted his head back and gazed down at them. "Oh," he muttered, "how I wish I could swim like them." Quickly realizing his feathers were no match for water, he devised a plan: he'd weave together tiny floats from seaweed seeds he found along the shore.

That afternoon, he took his new floats out for a test swim. Suddenly lifted into the air by the wind, he flew around the dolphins, impressing them immensely.

"Well done!" laughed a dolphin. "How did you ever manage that?"

"I just... thought," the crow began tentatively, "maybe sometimes you just float on air?"

They all chuckled. A wise old owl nearby praised the crow for his
